evals.prompt: Debug the Playwright test failures in evals/files/results-selector-timeout.json. Classify each failure and suggest fixes.
evals.expected_output: Should classify 2 failures: F1 (selector '#submit-btn' not found in checkout.spec.ts) and F2 (timeout 30000ms exceeded waiting for getByRole('dialog') in dialog.spec.ts). Should provide concrete fix suggestions for each — e.g., update selector to match current DOM for F1, increase timeout or verify dialog trigger for F2. Should note the 2 passing tests are unaffected.
assertions: Classifies '#submit-btn' failure as F1 (Selector not found)
Classifies dialog timeout as F2 (Timeout exceeded)
Identifies checkout.spec.ts:18 as location for selector failure
Identifies dialog.spec.ts:15 as location for timeout failure
Notes duration near 30000ms as timeout signal for F2
Suggests fix for F1: update selector or verify element exists in DOM
Suggests fix for F2: check dialog trigger or increase timeout
Reports 2 failed, 2 passed tests total
Structured output with severity ratings (P1/P2)
Summary table included

evals.prompt: Debug the Playwright test failures in evals/files/results-mixed-failures.json. Classify each failure, identify any cascading patterns, and suggest fixes.
evals.expected_output: Should classify 3 failures: F5 (API returns 500 in api-integration.spec.ts), F7 (session cookie expired causing redirect to /login in auth-session.spec.ts), F4 (assertion mismatch 'Welcome' vs 'Login' in welcome.spec.ts). Should detect potential cascade: F7 auth/session expiry could be the root cause of F4 assertion mismatch (user sees 'Login' instead of 'Welcome' because session expired).
assertions: Classifies API 500 error as F5 (Network/API failure)
Classifies session_expired redirect as F7 (Auth/session expired)
Classifies 'Welcome' vs 'Login' mismatch as F4 (Assertion mismatch)
Detects cascade pattern: F7 auth failure likely causes F4 assertion mismatch
Identifies net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED and 500 status in API failure
Identifies session_token cookie expiration in auth failure
Suggests fix for F5: mock API or verify staging server health
Suggests fix for F7: refresh auth state or extend session TTL
Suggests fix for F4: depends on resolving F7 first
Reports 3 failed, 0 passed tests total
Structured output with severity ratings
Summary table included

evals.prompt: Debug the Playwright test failures in evals/files/results-flaky.json. Identify flaky tests, race conditions, and suggest stabilization strategies.
evals.expected_output: Should identify flaky test patterns from retry data. 'notification count' fails retry 0, passes retry 1 — classic F8 race condition (value arrives late). 'revenue chart' fails retries 0-1, passes retry 2 — F12 intermittent with rendering delay. 'loading spinner' fails all 3 retries — NOT flaky, this is a consistent F8 race condition (spinner disappears before assertion). Should distinguish true flaky from consistent failures.
assertions: Identifies 'notification count' as flaky (fails then passes on retry)
Classifies notification test as F8 (Race condition) — value arrives late
Identifies 'revenue chart' as flaky (passes on retry 2)
Classifies chart test as F12 (Flaky/intermittent) with rendering delay
Identifies 'loading spinner' as NOT flaky — fails all 3 retries consistently
Classifies spinner test as F8 (Race condition) — element disappears before check
Suggests stabilization: use web-first assertions with auto-retry
Suggests stabilization: wait for specific state instead of fixed element
Notes retries config is set to 2
Structured output with severity ratings (P1 for flaky, P2 for consistent)
Summary table included

evals.prompt: Debug the Playwright test results in evals/files/results-clean.json. Report any failures found.
evals.expected_output: Should report all tests passed with no failures. Clean run across login.spec.ts, navigation.spec.ts, and search.spec.ts. No failure classifications needed. Should not produce false diagnoses or fabricated issues.
assertions: Reports all tests passed (0 failures)
Reports clean run status
Does NOT fabricate any failure classifications
Does NOT produce false F-code diagnoses
Does NOT suggest unnecessary fixes
Mentions total test count (7 passed)
Does NOT flag any test as flaky or problematic
